title: codegen targets native + adapters
manager: Native code emitters where a host deserves one, and adapters into the ecosystem's existing FSM libraries (XState and friends) — with golden traces, so "export to XState" means behaviorally certified on the declared subset, not "looks roughly similar." This is how FSL meets developers where they already are: it compiles to the runtime they use, and golden traces through the adapter's actual runtime prove the export behaves identically or cleanly refuses the parts it can't.
cs: targets = plugins vs the S3 architecture (surface/emit-utils/TARGET_META); adapter set fsl#563-577 + Amazon States (#496) + taskflow/circle per demand; refusal-by-default on unsupported semantics (`--allow-loss` explicit); golden traces through the adapter's actual runtime; FeatureComparison becomes a tested contract.
